---
title: "House Prices: Advanced Regression Techniques"
author:
  - name: Chris Endemann
    email: endemann@wisc.edu

date: 2024-09-12
date-format: long
image: "../../images/vincent-van-zalinge-mDohQISBnCk-unsplash.jpg"

categories:
  - Projects
  - ML Marathon
  - MLM24
  - Regression
  - Tabular
  - XGBoost
  - Feature engineering
  - Getting started

---

## About this project
The House Prices challenge is a classic Kaggle "Getting Started" competition featured in the [2024 Machine Learning Marathon (MLM24)](https://ml-marathon.wisc.edu/). It serves as an accessible entry point for participants new to ML competitions, emphasizing feature engineering, data cleaning, and regression techniques.

### Challenge design
- **Task**: Predict the final sale price of homes in Ames, Iowa using 79 explanatory variables describing various aspects of residential properties.
- **Domain**: Tabular regression -- a great introduction to structured data modeling.
- **Methods**: Feature engineering, random forests, gradient boosting (XGBoost, LightGBM), and ensemble methods are commonly used.
- **Difficulty**: Getting Started -- ideal for beginners learning the Kaggle workflow and core ML concepts.

### Links
- **Kaggle challenge**: [House Prices - Advanced Regression Techniques](https://www.kaggle.com/c/house-prices-advanced-regression-techniques)

## See also
- [**Model**: XGBoost](https://uw-madison-datascience.github.io/ML-X-Nexus/Toolbox/Models/XGBoost.html): XGBoost is one of the most popular models for tabular regression tasks like this challenge.

## Questions?
If you have any lingering questions about this project, please feel free to post to the [Nexus Q&A](https://github.com/UW-Madison-DataScience/ML-X-Nexus/discussions/categories/q-a) on GitHub. We will improve materials on this website as additional questions come in.
